sources_exist.note: All 7 URL-bearing citations resolved to accessible pages, and the record has auditable HTTP(S) provenance. Resolution alone does not cure the 5 claim-support failures documented below.

figures_match_sources.note: Only the Deltek adoption citation and CTA exit-multiple citation support their quoted figures. The VDCI page contains no 71%/2012 or 2009-2010 adoption figures; the Zweig owner page does not support owners_60plus_pct=0.42; the Morrissey Goodale page reports 243 first-half-2024 deals and 42% PE/PE-backed share rather than the quoted 2025 figures or 18 platforms; the Zweig 2025 page supports 4.28x but not the stored 4.5x normalized paid multiple; and Monograph supports 59% fixed fee, 23% T&M, and 32% versus 19% government/private T&M, but not a 45% public-revenue share, 18% cost-plus share, or a FAR Part 31 mechanism.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The within-role fractions, pi_dist=0.38, and pi_moat=0.45 are bounded and have concrete rationales, but score-affecting t50_years=3.0 relies on uncited 47th-study claims of 70% overall adoption and 38% measurable impact plus an unsupported BIM diffusion timeline. owners_60plus_pct=0.42 and active_consolidators=18 are also unsupported estimates that materially feed B.

rubric_consistent.note: The record correctly uses the frozen formulas and separates M&A evidence from V/A, but it applies the OW succession bonus without cited evidence of a succession shortage, uses an unsupported count of 18 PE-backed consolidators, and uses 4.5x as a normalized small-firm buy multiple without an evidenced market-rate-owner-comp conversion or evidence that it is a median actually paid. The Stage-4 BORDERLINE flag is a review trigger rather than an error, and the ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is acknowledged as non-blocking deterministic tolerance; neither resolves the substantive rubric failures.

cross_checks_honest.note: Uplift, terminal-value, and no-proxy responses are candid and directionally consistent. The pricing-model-deflation cross-check, however, states that about 45% of revenue is under public QBS/audited-overhead contracts and that the firm cannot retain savings as established facts, although the cited Monograph page does not establish that revenue share, cost-plus share, or FAR/AASHTO treatment. That unsupported premise is central to pi_dist and C.

source_audits.note: The Deltek release resolves and reports a survey of nearly 700 US and Canadian A&E firms; it explicitly states that 53% leveraged AI tools, up from 38% in the prior year. It supports the quoted tool-use figure, though not material workflow adoption.

The VDCI page resolves and gives a qualitative global history of BIM and national mandates, but contains no 71% adoption figure, no 2012 majority-use statistic, and no claim that US/AEC adoption crossed 50% in 2009-2010 from a near-zero 2002 base.

The Zweig page resolves and supports nearly 80% of owners looking to transition in the next decade and a 2024 value/EBITDA ratio of 4.23. It does not report an average CEO age of 58, an owners-age distribution, owners_60plus_pct=0.42, an internal-pipeline shortage, or the cited 82% internal-sale preference.

The page resolves but reports 243 US deals in the first half of 2024, 156 unique buyers/investors, and a 42% share for PE/PE-backed buyers. It does not support the quoted 500+ 2025 transactions, greater-than-50% PE-backed share, tripling of PE-backed platforms since 2018, named acquisition counts, or the score-affecting count of 18 active PE-backed platforms.

The URL redirects to and resolves as Zweig's 2025 valuation article, which reports value/EBITDA=4.28 for a median AEC firm and EBITDA of $1,357,946. It does not support the quoted CTA 5x-7x range, a median multiple actually paid, the stored 4.5x value, or the claimed market-rate-owner-comp normalization.

The CTA page resolves and explicitly gives 8x-11x EBITDA for $8M-$25M EBITDA mid-size multi-state engineering platforms and 10x-13x+ for $25M+ premium platforms. The stored 8.5x is within the directly stated platform range, although the page is an adviser guide rather than disclosed transaction-level evidence.

The Monograph page resolves and supports 59% lump-sum/fixed-fee, 23% T&M, and government T&M use of 32% versus 19% for private clients. It does not report an 18% cost-plus share, a 45% public/government revenue share, or FAR Part 31/AASHTO audit treatment; it also states that fixed-fee efficiency stays with the firm.

reasons: $.inputs.historical_analogs.url cites https://vdci.edu/blog/the-evolution-and-adoption-of-bim-software-around-the-world for exact 71%/2012 and 2009-2010 BIM-adoption figures that the page does not contain. Re-run with an accessible primary or traceable survey source that directly establishes the adoption denominator, geography, years, and diffusion interval used in t50_years.

$.inputs.t50_years.derivation relies on uncited 47th Deltek-study claims of 70% overall adoption and 38% measurable impact, while https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/ai-talent-and-record-profits-what-the-46th-annual-deltek-clarity-ae-study-reveals-about-the-architecture--engineering-industry-302453217.html supports only the 46th-study 53% tool-use figure. Re-run with a directly accessible source for the material-adoption baseline and a supported historical analog.

$.inputs.owners_60plus_pct.value=0.42 and $.inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented.value=true are not supported by https://zweiggroup.com/blogs/news/zweig-group-releases-2024-valuation-report-of-aec-firms-highlighting-industry-growth-and-value-trends, which reports transition intentions but no owner-age distribution or successor shortage. Re-run with direct AEC owner-age and succession-pipeline evidence, and apply the bonus only if shortage is documented.

$.inputs.active_consolidators.value=18 is not supported by https://www.morrisseygoodale.com/resources/ae-annual-review/2024-ae-industry-ma-mid-year-review/, which reports 2024 deal and buyer totals but does not enumerate 18 qualifying PE-backed platforms active in the last 24 months. Re-run with an auditable platform-by-platform count, sponsor status, qualifying add-on, and date.

$.inputs.buy_mult.value=4.5 is not supported by https://zweiggroup.com/blogs/news/aec-valuations-remain-strong: the page reports a 4.28 value/EBITDA benchmark, not a median multiple actually paid or a demonstrated market-rate-owner-comp normalization. Re-run with transaction evidence for the in-band small-firm population and show the required owner-comp conversion.

$.inputs.pricing_structure.value and $.inputs.pi_dist.rationale use a roughly 45% public/government share and FAR/AASHTO cost pass-through that https://monograph.com/blog/engineering-fees-pricing-models-best-practices does not establish. Re-run with direct evidence for client/revenue mix and the applicable contract mechanisms, then ground pi_dist and the pricing cross-check in those supported scopes.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S, V
